tweets.text: .@POTUS Biden: We will repair our alliances and engage with the world once again. https://t.co/o2iJjg6xHQ https://t.co/EflTysgWlS
.@SecBlinken: America’s leadership is needed around the world, and we will provide it. https://t.co/0MMuiwjS6w https://t.co/Cnki4s0gnH

Antony J. Blinken walked through the doors of the Department for the first time 28 years ago as a regional bureau special assistant. On January 27, he entered as the 71st Secretary of State. Read about @SecBlinken’s first day in our DipNote blog. https://t.co/Ub9T8Ynevw

On January 27, the Department welcomed back former Deputy Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken as our 71st U.S. Secretary of State. For the latest news on the Secretary, follow him on Twitter @SecBlinken and visit https://t.co/58Uc9lZRv7. https://t.co/jznQjRviTg

.@SecBlinken is hitting the ground running. Follow the Secretary on Twitter to read about his latest activities, calls, meetings, future travel and all things U.S. foreign policy. https://t.co/QZcrZxK6gM

.@SecBlinken: [Journalists] keep the American people and the world informed about what we do here. That’s key to our mission as well. And you hold us accountable, ask tough questions, and that really does make us better. https://t.co/4oC7i2CGZj

.@SecBlinken: A free press is a cornerstone of our democracy. And this is a critical moment for protecting and defending democracy, including right here at home. https://t.co/PQ4ouMmroY https://t.co/JTjlyAegex
.@SecBlinken: When it comes to foreign policy, it is hard to have a sustainable foreign policy absent the informed consent of the American people. https://t.co/0A2Z3aDxWj

.@ClimateEnvoy Kerry at the #AdaptationSummit: “Only together are we going to be able to build the resilience to climate change that is critical to save lives and meet our moral obligation to future generations.” https://t.co/DdHgslrpS4 https://t.co/HSOdw6tDA8
.@SecBlinken discusses the Biden Administration's review of recent actions in Russia. https://t.co/puBVPLibbn
.@SecBlinken: The relationship between the United States and China is arguably the most important relationship that we have in the world going forward. https://t.co/j7u56AQpGJ
.@SecBlinken discusses Iran, the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action, and the @POTUS Biden Administration’s approach to attaining a longer and stronger agreement. https://t.co/Am674ct5rA

.@SecBlinken at his Senate confirmation hearing: No single country acting alone, even one as powerful as the United States, can fully and effectively address these problems. https://t.co/7GyQ0c3hHx
.@SecBlinken at his Senate confirmation hearing: We will revitalize American diplomacy to deal with and take on the most pressing challenges of our time. https://t.co/jTe2rvHZsY

.@SecBlinken at his Senate confirmation hearing: I am committed to advancing our security and prosperity by building a diplomatic corps that fully represents America in all its talent and in all its diversity. https://t.co/4C93HBw46m
.@SecBlinken: America at its best still has a greater capacity than any other nation on earth to mobilize others for the better. https://t.co/0MMuiwjS6w https://t.co/IbkNdorghM

.@SecBlinken: America’s values are noble and powerful – and we will recommit to them. And America’s leadership is needed around the world, and we will provide it, because the world is far more likely to solve problems and meet challenges when the United States is there. https://t.co/MKFbh61ULn

.@SecBlinken: We must invest significantly in building a diverse and inclusive State Department. We cannot do our job of advancing America’s interests, values, and commitment to democracy without a State Department that is truly representative of the American people. https://t.co/dlCeBAoHHM

.@SecBlinken: To all the State Department personnel worldwide and to the women and men of @USAID, whose work exemplifies America at its best – I want you to know how proud I am to be on your team. https://t.co/vxXhTV0Ufq

Upholding the right to religious freedom is a moral and national security imperative. Where religious freedom is absent, terrorism and violence fester. Our advocacy for religious communities abroad helps to ensure the protection and prosperity of Americans at home. https://t.co/ETJtZ9qRXf

.@State_E Under Secretary Krach announces the 2020 #SecStateACE Award for Corporate Excellence, which recognizes U.S. businesses as responsible partners that add value to the communities where they operate abroad, while furthering economic growth and security in the U.S. https://t.co/Xf3aiqjmyw

Over 50 of our partners – representing over two-thirds of the world’s GDP – have joined the Clean Network as Clean Countries. They have rejected telcos beholden to the Chinese Communist Party and instead will work with trusted 5G vendors. Learn more at https://t.co/SXzHsiErNi. https://t.co/6RfBKu6ZID

The Clean Network now includes 180 telecommunications companies and dozens of leading high-tech companies as part our international effort to protect citizens’ privacy and companies’ sensitive information from intrusions by malign actors. Learn more: https://t.co/SXzHsiErNi. https://t.co/rdmD4A05HR

The Clean Network initiative is a comprehensive effort to address the long-term threat to data privacy, security, and human rights in the free world posed by authoritarian malign actors such as the Chinese Communist Party. https://t.co/x1jdhOOcsu https://t.co/8AKi93ghGZ
What can you do to make a difference for National Slavery and Human Trafficking Prevention Month? You can start here: https://t.co/UrWEbhScn9. #EndTrafficking

At this very moment, human traffickers are robbing 25 million people of their freedom and basic human dignity. This is a global epidemic. It is our collective responsibility to extinguish human trafficking wherever it exists. #EndTrafficking https://t.co/zmYmoej90e

We are proud to announce a new whole-of-government human trafficking website with federal resources for victims, current funding opportunities, public awareness and training efforts and information on survivor leadership. https://t.co/DSLJoW6j81

Our new webpage documents the Chinese Communist Party’s blatant disregard for human rights. The United States will always stand up for those​ who are denied their fundamental human rights, including freedom of religion or belief and expression. https://t.co/YXn7t4jaZB

Since 2017, more than one million individuals have disappeared into the Chinese Communist Party’s internment camps in Xinjiang. Many families still have no idea if their loved ones are even alive. We are sharing their stories at https://t.co/YTsl27KzK5. https://t.co/4AOMMtAfTq

Upholding the right to religious freedom is a moral and national security imperative. Where religious freedom is absent, terrorism and violence fester. Our advocacy for religious communities abroad helps to ensure the protection and prosperity of Americans at home. https://t.co/IQGnjpFxLy

.@SecPompeo: The momentum in favor of secure 5G is building. Countries need to be able to trust that 5G equipment and software won't threaten national security, economic security, privacy, intellectual property, or human rights. https://t.co/ihiraM8MDE https://t.co/2UsIxSCdqZ

The tide is turning against Huawei and other surveillance tools of the Chinese Communist Party. Many countries and companies are choosing to allow only trusted vendors in their 5G networks. We call on our freedom-loving partners to join the Clean Network. https://t.co/478U23JMkh

Countries around the world are coming together to secure their 5G infrastructure from untrusted vendors. Read in @StateMag how Bulgaria, North Macedonia, and Kosovo in one day all joined the Clean Network coalition. https://t.co/Mzz3rWczsE
